generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Spatial sensor embedded within BIM environment to detect human presence in rooms, enabling context-aware data collection for lighting system automation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance LightingSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor integrated into BIM model to monitor lighting conditions (illuminance levels) within building spaces."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor spatially aligned with HVAC units to provide real-time environmental data for building control system optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity sensor embedded in BIM model to monitor moisture levels, supporting adaptive control of ventilation systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance LightingDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingSensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACTemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalHumiditySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BCSEnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM model containing Building Control System components (sensors, actuators, controllers) as defined by Building Control Engineers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler
]

instance RuntimeEnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time data stream combining processed sensor inputs (occupancy, lighting, temperature, humidity) synchronized with physical building states."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, LightingDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model defining automated control responses (e.g., adaptive lighting based on occupancy) implemented within BIM environment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge data ingestion layer for real-time sensor data from physical building systems (e.g., lighting sensors) via IoT connectivity."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Device twin management platform providing dynamic registration, state synchronization, and event handling for Building Control System components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, ControlLogicService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization engine enabling Facility Managers and DT Engineers to interact with 3D representations of building systems and sensor networks."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance BIMIntegrationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Bridging layer enabling direct modeling of BCS components within BIM environments, transforming static representations into dynamic DT artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, ControlLogicService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core MDE framework automating extraction of structured DT artifacts from enriched BIM models with minimal manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:enables LifecycleManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling Building Control Engineers to define sensors, actuators, and controllers directly within BIM models, creating unified semantic foundations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceModelingInsight
]

instance RealTimeDataExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring continuous synchronization between physical buildings and Digital Twin via Eclipse Hono and Ditto platforms."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing immersive 3D interfaces for monitoring and controlling building systems through Unreal Engine visualizations."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualDiagnosticsAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling DT Engineers to integrate custom analytics plugins via modular architecture's standardized extension points."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight
]

instance ControlLogicService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service allowing Building Control Engineers to implement and deploy control logic within BIM-based Digital Twin for automated physical system responses."
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveLightingAction
]

instance LifecycleManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service orchestrating entire DT deployment lifecycle from device modeling to runtime operation, ensuring seamless transitions between stages."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DeviceModelingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic foundation created through automated extraction of BCS components from enriched BIM models for subsequent DT deployment."
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous synchronization state between physical building systems and Digital Twin, enabling Facility Managers to monitor operational conditions."
]

instance VisualDiagnosticsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Immersive exploration capability allowing Facility Managers to diagnose building system states through 3D visualizations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analytics-driven insights enabling proactive maintenance scheduling based on real-time sensor data and system performance trends."
]

instance AdaptiveLightingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control response system adjusting lighting configurations based on occupancy and environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeploymentValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification of model correctness during MDE transformation phases ensuring consistency between BIM and Digital Twin representations."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Configurable control element within BIM model enabling remote operation of lighting systems through Facility Manager interfaces."
]

instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control device integrated into Digital Twin for remote actuation of HVAC systems based on environmental sensor inputs."
]

instance VentilationActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Control mechanism for adaptive ventilation systems, synchronized with humidity and temperature sensor data through Ditto platform."
]

// C1: Environment
instance BuildingControlSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Smart building infrastructure environment containing BCS components and their Digital Twin representations."
    base:contains OccupancySensor, LightingSensor, HVACTemperatureSensor, EnvironmentalHumiditySensor,
                 LightingActuator, HVACActuator, VentilationActuator,
                 BCSEnrichedBIMModel, ControlLogicModel
]

// C5: Data Relationships
instance SensorDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Standardized data stream combining all transmitted sensor data for processing by runtime systems."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, LightingDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

// C7: Component Relationships
instance BCSComponentContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Container component representing the hierarchical organization of Building Control System elements within the Digital Twin."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingControlSystemEnvironment
]

// C8: Data Processing
instance ProcessedEnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized and filtered environmental data prepared for visualization and control logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineEnabler, ControlLogicService
]

// C12: System Under Study
instance BuildingControlSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Building Control System focusing on lighting systems automation and integration with physical infrastructure via Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment BuildingControlSystemEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control flows from the Digital Twin to physical systems are enabled through **bidirectional data exchange**, where the framework acts as a central hub for **operational commands** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control) sent back to Building Control Systems (BCS). The system supports **real-time monitoring and control** for Facility Managers, though specific **command types, message formats (e.g., JSON/XML), validation mechanisms, or authentication protocols** are not described in the documents. Feedback systems are implied via the **runtime configuration management** feature, allowing adjustments without redeployment, but **conflict resolution strategies or command execution confirmations** remain unspecified."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The document emphasizes the need for **real-time synchronization** between physical assets and their digital twin representations to enable data-driven infrastructure management and optimization. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed, the context implies that **low-latency, high-frequency data streams** are critical for predictive analytics and decision-making across the building lifecycle (e.g., design to operation). Temporal granularity must align with operational needs, such as monitoring asset performance, detecting anomalies, or simulating dynamic scenarios (e.g., energy consumption, structural integrity). The document highlights the challenge of integrating **heterogeneous runtime data** with static models, suggesting that temporal alignment is a key enabler for seamless DT functionality, though specific time-scale constraints (e.g., millisecond vs. hourly updates) are not explicitly outlined."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The document outlines that Digital Twins in the **AECO domain** require **carefully designed integration of static models with runtime data** to create a virtual representation for simulation, analysis, and optimization. While no explicit **architecture patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or **component integration frameworks** are mentioned, the text implies a need for **system-wide coordination mechanisms** to manage data-driven infrastructure management. The **orchestration** likely involves linking physical sensors, IoT devices, and enterprise systems, though the specifics of **resource management** (e.g., scalability, fault tolerance) or **system-wide governance** are not detailed. The focus is on enabling **predictive analytics and enhanced decision-making**, suggesting a **modular, extensible architecture** to adapt to heterogeneous environments."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    The framework supports the **complete life cycle of Digital Twin (DT) deployment and operation**, addressing the gap between BIM’s static representations and the dynamic nature of DTs. Key phases include:

    1. **Device Modeling**:
       - **Representation**: BIM models are enriched to include Building Control System (BCS) components (sensors, actuators, controllers) by Building Control Engineers.
       - **Transition**: Manual configuration and logic definition within BIM, transitioning to structured representations via Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles.
       - **Governance**: Governed by functional requirements (e.g., F_X in Table III) to ensure semantic consistency and interoperability.

    2. **DT Deployment**:
       - **Representation**: Automated generation of deployable DT artifacts from enriched BIM models, minimizing manual intervention.
       - **Transition**: MDE-driven extraction of structured data for deployment, ensuring alignment between physical and digital systems.
       - **Governance**: Modular architecture with standardized extension points to support scalability and adaptability.

    3. **Operation and Monitoring**:
       - **Representation**: Real-time synchronization between physical infrastructure and the DT, with interactive visualization for Facility Managers.
       - **Transition**: Continuous data exchange enables dynamic updates to the DT, reflecting operational changes.
       - **Governance**: Role-based access (e.g., Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and lifecycle management for operational continuity.

    4. **Customization and Evolution**:
       - **Representation**: Integration of custom services/plugins by DT Engineers to extend DT functionality.
       - **Transition**: Modular design allows seamless addition of new services, adapting to evolving requirements.
       - **Governance**: Version control and modular updates ensure controlled evolution without disrupting core operations.

    **Stakeholders Involved**:
    - Building Control Engineers (modeling/control logic),
    - Facility Managers (monitoring/operation),
    - DT Engineers (customization/extension).
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The framework addresses **fidelity and validity** through a **model-driven lifecycle approach**, grounded in research projects **TwinLight** and **BOREALIS**. Key considerations include:

- **Model fidelity requirements**: Achieved via **automated extraction** of domain semantics from BIM models, ensuring alignment between static BIM representations and dynamic DT operations. The framework mitigates BIM’s limitations (e.g., static component definitions) by generating **runtime interfaces** for real-time synchronization.
- **Validation and verification**: Implicitly supported through the **semantic integration** of BIM and operational data, though explicit **validation methods** (e.g., formal testing or benchmarking) are not detailed. The framework references **validating the model** in Section IV, suggesting iterative refinement during deployment.
- **Uncertainty sources**: Not explicitly quantified in the provided documents, though **data fragmentation** and **semantic drift** are noted in related work (not as framework features). The system does not describe **error detection/correction mechanisms** or **quality assurance processes** beyond automated artifact generation.
- **Real-time data exchange**: Ensures continuous synchronization between physical and digital states, reducing temporal discrepancies but not addressing **uncertainty propagation** or **sensor noise** explicitly.
"
]

]

    base:desc "The Digital Twin deployment framework supports **model-based deployment** of operational DTs, transitioning from **enriched BIM models** to runtime systems. Deployment models are implied to be **low-overhead and maintainable**, but hardware/software specifications remain unspecified."

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems** through **standardized frameworks** such as **Building Information Modeling (BIM)** and **real-time data exchange platforms** like **Eclipse Hono and Ditto**. These tools enable **interoperability** with legacy and modern systems, ensuring seamless data flow across the **AECO domain**. The architecture supports **real-time data exchange** with external stakeholders and enterprise systems, though specific **data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or **integration patterns** (e.g., API-based connectors, message brokers) are not explicitly mentioned. The focus remains on **real-time synchronization** and **collaborative decision-making** across heterogeneous environments."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The framework adheres to **BIM as the standard solution** in the construction industry, leveraging it as the foundational data model for DT deployment. **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles are central to standardization, enabling **automated transformations** from BIM to deployable DT artifacts. The framework also emphasizes **standardized extension points** for integrating custom services, though specific **industry standards** (e.g., ISO 42010, OPC UA) or **certification frameworks** are not referenced.

Interoperability is addressed through **modular architecture** and **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** (mentioned as part of the framework’s ecosystem), but no explicit **protocol standards** (e.g., MQTT, REST) or **interoperability specifications** are detailed. The text highlights a **lack of standardized integration frameworks** in current BIM-DT ecosystems, positioning the proposed framework as an attempt to mitigate this gap through its modular design.
"
]
